info.title: Greenhouse Platform — Read API (Phase 3 optimizer planning-context read path)
info.description: The platform Go API's Phase 3 telemetry read path (catalog contract #7) — the request/response contract the optimizer (Phase 3) uses to read one greenhouse's planning context from the platform's history. One endpoint: `GET /api/greenhouses/{greenhouse_id}/planning-context`, returning current setpoints, bucketed telemetry summaries, latest actuator states, and data-quality/freshness signals in a single bounded read per planning cycle. This is the optimizer's read counterpart to its setpoint write path (`optimizer-platform-setpoints-rest`, catalog #3). Per the revised RFC-008 (ADR 2026-07-07), the cross-service boundary is REST: the platform may back these handlers with internal SQL views or TimescaleDB continuous aggregates, but those database objects are platform-internal implementation details, not the contract, and the optimizer never connects to the database directly. History is returned as `(min, mean, max)` summaries per metric per bucket (RFC-004), not raw readings, keeping the payload bounded. The response carries a top-level `schema_version` — unlike the write contracts — because the optimizer's input-gate identity-consistency check reads it to detect read-API/contract drift. Identity, units, and timestamps follow RFC-007: the same `greenhouse_id` slug that keys MQTT topics, controller REST paths, and DB rows; field names are snake_case; timestamps are RFC 3339 UTC, millisecond precision. The read carries no authority and no safety concern, so it is unauthenticated on the trusted Docker network — RFC-011 scopes config-gated service auth to the write boundaries, not this read. `info.version` is the contract version; its major bumps on a breaking change, recorded in an ADR (RFC-007), and `schema_version` tracks that major. This document is split: the single path is a file under `paths/`, and shared schemas, parameters, and responses live under `components/`; run `redocly bundle` to emit a single self-contained file.

servers.description: The platform's nginx entry point, which reverse-proxies `/api` to the Go API. The optimizer reaches this read path over the internal Docker network.
